mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction in backtests with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and capital demand channels, driving sustained corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over the next month when the economy is not constrained by inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3%, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of aggregate income growth and consumer spending power, systematically supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS levels against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.01, or directional accuracy drops to 50% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirms robust domestic demand fundamentals, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the interaction term in a multivariate regression over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and stable or rising unemployment signals a supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The negative signal from the INDPRO-UNRATE divergence produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.
